title: coelogyne protracta
intro: Coelogyne protracta, also known as the pendant orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of India. This plant is used in traditional medicine and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Coelogyne protracta include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
